summary: Frontend testing of UX restructuring (4-tab nav, Connect page merger, Security Log dropdown). 17/17 functional features verified working on both desktop (1920x1080) and mobile (390x844) viewports for both admin and guest users.
passed_tests: Desktop admin nav shows exactly 4 tabs: Home, Connect, Me, Manage (data-testids: nav-home, nav-connect, nav-me, nav-manage)
Desktop guest nav shows exactly 3 tabs: Home, Connect, Me (no Manage)
Mobile guest bottom-tab-bar shows 3 tabs: Home, Connect, Me
Mobile admin bottom-tab-bar shows 4 tabs: Home, Connect, Me, Manage (visually confirmed via screenshot)
Header search icon (header-search-btn) navigates to /search
Mobile search icon (mobile-search-btn) present and functional
Avatar dropdown for admin shows Settings + Security Log links
Security Log link from desktop dropdown navigates to /security-log
Mobile admin avatar dropdown shows Security Log
Guest avatar dropdown does NOT show Security Log (correct)
Connect page loads with Group Chat tab active by default (connect-tab-chat highlighted)
Connect page Direct Messages tab activates on click (connect-tab-direct)
Group Chat tab shows message input field
/chat route redirects to /connect
/messages route redirects to /connect?tab=direct (Direct Messages tab auto-activated via URL param)
Home tab navigates to /dashboard (shows courses)
Me tab navigates to /progress
Manage tab navigates to /admin for admin users
Guest banner displays on dashboard for guest users (guest-banner data-testid)
Mobile header is slim (57px height) with no horizontal scrolling tab strip
critical_code_review_comments: Layout.jsx lines 107-120: navItems correctly built with 3 base tabs + conditional Manage tab for isTeacherOrAdmin
Layout.jsx lines 153-186: dropdownContent shared between desktop and mobile dropdowns; Security Log link gated by isAdmin (correct)
Layout.jsx lines 326-353: Mobile bottom-tab-bar uses fixed positioning with safe-area-inset-bottom padding; main content has pb-20 padding to clear bar
Connect.jsx lines 11-13: tab state initialized from URL searchParams correctly (?tab=direct activates Direct Messages tab)
App.js lines 235-242: /chat and /messages routes use Navigate component with replace=true for clean redirects
Layout.jsx line 110: 'Me' tab match array includes both /progress and /settings, so Settings link in dropdown will keep Me tab highlighted (good UX)
Console warnings about jotai atomFamily deprecation - non-blocking, low priority for upgrade later
WebSocket warnings on /api/ws/chat are non-blocking (chat falls back to HTTP polling)

test_credentials.guest: Try Demo button on landing page (POST /api/auth/guest)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 1+2 of UX restructuring is fully verified. Layout.jsx renders 4 core tabs (Home/Connect/Me/Manage) on desktop top nav AND mobile bottom-tab-bar. Manage tab only shows for isTeacherOrAdmin. Connect.jsx merges Chat (Group Chat) + Messages (Direct Messages) with tabbed interface; reads ?tab= URL param for deep linking. Security Log link added to shared dropdown content (visible to admin in both desktop and mobile dropdowns). Old /chat redirects to /connect, /messages redirects to /connect?tab=direct. Mobile header is slim 57px without horizontal scroll tab strip. Test data-testids: desktop-nav, bottom-tab-bar, nav-home, nav-connect, nav-me, nav-manage, bottom-nav-{label}, header-search-btn, mobile-search-btn, desktop-avatar-menu, mobile-avatar-menu, connect-page, connect-tabs, connect-tab-chat, connect-tab-direct, guest-banner.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. Three test runner errors (admin login timeout, manage nav not visible, mobile admin login timeout) were Playwright timing issues - admin login redirects to '/' (HomeRoute) which then renders Dashboard, not directly to /dashboard, so wait_for_url('**/dashboard') timed out even though login succeeded. Subsequent tests still passed because the page WAS on dashboard. The mobile_admin.png screenshot visually confirms 4 bottom tabs are present for admin user.
